summary: STSVK framework reasoning is implemented in the Oracle Engine (Phase 3 formal path). Conversational chat has access to the 481 seed DTUs containing constraint geometry and repair dominance content.

pipeline_phases: analyze() — classify query, pick domain + task type
retrieve() — pull relevant DTUs + context from lattice
compute() — synthesize answer; run STSVK constraint check if formal
cite() — attach source DTUs and theorem citations
validate() — confidence scoring including STSVK constraint score
record() — persist result as oracle_answer DTU
oracle_engine.oracle_prompt: Explicitly instructs LLM to: cite DTU sources by ID, include computation traces for formal claims, mark claims as KNOWN/PROBABLE/UNCERTAIN/UNKNOWN, suggest follow-up questions, never contradict computationalGroundTruth

stsvk_framework.description: 480 theorems + 1 root fixed-point identity = seed DTU lattice
stsvk_framework.cache: STSVK theorem cache (_stsvkCache) loaded lazily on first formal compute()

chat_integration.formal_mode: Oracle Phase 3 runs STSVK constraint checks for formal queries
chat_integration.conversational_mode: Chat has access to constraint geometry DTUs through semantic retrieval; framework language appears when query matches DTU content

gaps.description: Framework-grounded reasoning (explicit STSVK constraint checking) only activates in Oracle mode (formal queries). Conversational chat does not explicitly invoke constraint geometry verification, though the underlying DTU content is available.
gaps.note: This is a design choice, not a bug. Oracle mode is the formal-reasoning path. Chat mode is conversational.
